semanticCore: Wandering aimlessly, extreme thirst, camel disease, love-struck, or sand
quranicRelevance: Poets wandering aimlessly (Yaheemoon - 'fee kulli wadin yaheemoon'), and thirsty camels ('al-heem').

keyDerivations.meaning: To wander aimlessly, lose path, rove without target, or be distracted
Thirsty camels, dry sand absorbing water, or extremely dehydrated beasts

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَشَارِبُونَ شُرْبَ الْهِيمِ (56:55): 'And drinking like the drinking of thirsty camels' (the agonizing thirst of Hell dwellers).
أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِي كُلِّ وَادٍ يَهِيمُونَ (26:225): Do you not see that they (poets) wander aimlessly in every valley? (erratic speech patterns).
discardedNoise: Deep love obsession (Huyam) in romance stories.
summaryEn: The root represents aimless wandering and burning thirst (Hyam), describing erratic poetic speech (wandering valleys) and the painful drinking of the damned like thirsty camels (Heem).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر هيمان العطش الشديد للإبل وهائم الكلام بلا مقصد (هيم)، واصفاً ارتكاس الشعراء في الخيال الكاذب، وشرب الكفار لماء الحميم كالإبل الظامئة.
rawText: مَهْيُومٌ : see voce قَتَّ. هَيَامٌ : see an ex. in a verse of Lebeed, voce أَصْلٌ. هَيَّمَهُ 2 : see هُيُومٌ. هَيْمَانُ A man loving intensely, or very passionately or fondly: (TA:) and so applied to a heart: see شَنِقٌ. === هَيْمَانُ A thirsty camel: fem. هَيْمَى: pl. هِيمٌ. (Jel, lvi. 55.) هُيَامٌ : see هُبٌّ. هُيُومٌ [The being bewildered, or distracted, by amorous desire;] the pursuing a heedless, or an inconsiderate course, or going at random, heedlessly, or without consideration, or certain aim, by reason of amorous desire: and تَهْيَامٌ the [being so, or] doing so, much. (TA.) You say, of love [or amorous desire], 🡻هَيَّمَهُ, inf. n. تَهْيِيمٌ. (TA.) === An affection like insanity arising from amorous desire: (JK:) bewilderment, or distraction, by amorous desire. (KL.)
